questions.question: A 200-pound object is pushed 10 feet across a flat floor. How much work is done if the pushing force is 80 pounds?
questions.explanation: Work = Force × Distance. The pushing force is 80 lb (not the weight). W = 80 × 10 = 800 ft·lb.

questions.question: Which of Newton’s laws explains why you feel pushed back in your seat when a vehicle accelerates?
questions.explanation: First Law (inertia): your body resists the change in motion when the vehicle accelerates.

questions.question: A gear with 25 teeth drives a gear with 75 teeth. If the driver turns at 600 RPM, the driven gear turns at:
In a hydraulic press, the input piston has an area of 4 in² and the output piston has an area of 40 in². If 30 pounds is applied to the input, what force is produced at the output?
Which type of simple machine is a doorstop?
questions.explanation: A doorstop is a wedge — two inclined planes back to back.

questions.question: A 10-foot ramp rises 2.5 feet. What is the mechanical advantage?
Static friction is:
questions.explanation: Static friction is greater than kinetic friction for the same surfaces.

questions.question: A compound pulley system has 6 rope segments supporting the load. To lift 600 pounds, you need:
In Ohm’s Law (V = IR), if voltage is 240V and resistance is 60 ohms, the current is:
Two belted pulleys rotate in:
questions.explanation: Belt-connected pulleys rotate in the same direction.

questions.question: An object’s momentum equals:
A crane hook moves upward at a constant speed. Its acceleration is:
Which material is the best insulator?
questions.explanation: Rubber is an insulator. Copper, aluminum, and steel are conductors.

questions.question: A lever has an effort arm of 8 feet and a load arm of 2 feet. Its MA is:
Two resistors of 20 ohms each are wired in parallel. Total resistance is:
A ball is thrown straight up. At the top of its path:
choices: Both speed and acceleration are zero
questions.explanation: At the top, velocity is zero momentarily. Acceleration due to gravity is still 32 ft/s² (not zero).

questions.question: Pressure increases when:
choices: Force decreases and area stays the same
Force stays the same and area decreases
questions.explanation: P = F/A. Same force with less area = more pressure.

questions.question: A 30-tooth driver meshes with a 30-tooth driven gear. The driven gear turns:
choices: At the same speed as the driver
questions.explanation: Same number of teeth = same speed, opposite direction.

questions.question: Kinetic energy depends on:
questions.explanation: KE = ½ mv² depends on mass and velocity.

questions.question: An idler gear placed between a driver and driven gear:
choices: Reverses the direction of the driven gear
questions.explanation: An idler gear reverses the direction of the driven gear (so driver and driven turn the same way).

questions.question: A worker pushes a 100-lb crate on a floor with coefficient of kinetic friction 0.4. The friction force is:
Power is measured in:
questions.explanation: Power is measured in watts or foot-pounds per second.

questions.question: In a series circuit with three 15-ohm resistors, total resistance is:
Buoyancy force equals:
questions.explanation: Buoyancy = weight of displaced fluid (Archimedes’ principle).

questions.question: A screw is fundamentally a:
choices: Inclined plane wrapped around a cylinder
questions.explanation: A screw is an inclined plane wrapped around a cylinder.

questions.question: Doubling an object’s velocity has what effect on its kinetic energy?
questions.explanation: KE = ½ mv². Doubling v: ½ m(2v)² = ½ m × 4v² = 4 times the KE.

questions.question: A wheelbarrow is an example of which class of lever?
questions.explanation: Wheelbarrow: load between fulcrum (wheel) and effort (handles) = second class.

questions.question: When a pipe narrows, fluid velocity:
questions.explanation: Continuity: fluid speeds up when the pipe narrows.

questions.question: An object accelerates from 0 to 40 ft/s in 8 seconds. Its acceleration is:
A belt-driven system has a 10-inch driver pulley and a 5-inch driven pulley. If the driver turns at 200 RPM, the driven turns at:
questions.explanation: Ratio = 10/5 = 2. Driven turns twice as fast: 200 × 2 = 400 RPM.

questions.question: Conservation of energy states that energy:
choices: Cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed
questions.explanation: Conservation of energy: energy cannot be created or destroyed.

questions.question: A 120V circuit with 15A draws how much power?
Which simple machine would best help change the direction of a pulling force?
questions.explanation: A fixed pulley changes the direction of force (pull down to lift up).

questions.question: Newton’s Third Law states that forces always come in:
questions.explanation: Newton’s Third Law: equal and opposite pairs.

questions.question: A larger gear meshing with a smaller gear will cause the smaller gear to:
questions.explanation: Smaller gear turns faster with less torque.

questions.question: A hydraulic system multiplies force because:
choices: Pressure is transmitted equally through confined fluid
questions.explanation: Pascal’s principle: pressure transmits equally through confined fluid.

questions.question: An object weighing 50 lbs falls from a 10-ft height. Its potential energy at the top is:
Lubricant reduces friction by:
questions.explanation: Lubricant creates a thin film between surfaces to reduce direct contact.

questions.question: In a parallel circuit, if one branch fails:
questions.explanation: In parallel, other branches continue if one fails.

questions.question: A driver walks 40 ft east then 30 ft north. Displacement is:
Work is done ONLY when:
choices: Force causes movement in the direction of the force
questions.explanation: Work requires force causing movement in the direction of the force.

questions.question: Rolling friction is typically:
questions.explanation: Rolling friction < kinetic friction < static friction.

questions.question: A first-class lever has the fulcrum:
questions.explanation: First-class lever: fulcrum between effort and load.

questions.question: Gravity accelerates all objects at the same rate regardless of:
questions.explanation: In a vacuum, gravity accelerates all objects equally regardless of mass, shape, or any other property.

questions.question: What defines a confined space according to the passage?
choices: Any space smaller than 100 sq ft
A space large enough to enter with limited access and not designed for continuous occupancy
questions.explanation: The passage defines it as a space large enough to enter, with limited access, not designed for continuous occupancy.

questions.question: What is the normal oxygen concentration in air?
questions.explanation: The passage states normal oxygen concentration is 20.9%.

questions.question: What must happen before a worker enters a confined space?
choices: A fire extinguisher must be placed inside
questions.explanation: The atmosphere must be tested using a calibrated four-gas monitor.

questions.question: Why is the attendant prohibited from entering the confined space?
choices: To prevent additional casualties if conditions are dangerous
It is not part of their job description
questions.explanation: To prevent additional casualties — the attendant must summon rescue rather than enter the dangerous space.

questions.question: An oxygen level of 18% would be classified as:
questions.explanation: Below 19.5% is deficient according to OSHA. 18% is below that threshold.

questions.question: The passage mentions a four-gas monitor. What does it detect?
choices: Oxygen, combustible gases, carbon monoxide, hydrogen sulfide
questions.explanation: Oxygen, combustible gases, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen sulfide.

questions.question: According to the passage, which is an example of engulfment?
choices: Being trapped by grain or sand
questions.explanation: Engulfment is being trapped by loose materials like grain or sand.

questions.question: The author’s purpose in writing this passage is to:
choices: Inform readers about confined space hazards and procedures
Compare confined spaces to open-air worksites
questions.explanation: The passage informs readers about confined space hazards and required safety procedures.

questions.question: Which welding process is most commonly used in structural steel construction?
questions.explanation: SMAW (stick welding) is most widely used in structural steel construction.

questions.question: Why is MIG welding popular in manufacturing?
choices: It is fast and easy to use
questions.explanation: MIG welding is prized for its speed and ease of use.

questions.question: What is arc flash?
choices: Eye burns caused by UV light from the welding arc
questions.explanation: Arc flash is eye burns caused by intense UV light from the arc.

questions.question: How long must a fire watch be maintained after welding?
questions.explanation: Fire watch must be maintained for at least 30 minutes.

questions.question: Which welding process produces the most precise welds?
questions.explanation: TIG welding produces the cleanest, most precise welds.

questions.question: The word “versatility” as used in the passage most likely means:
choices: Ability to be used in many different situations
questions.explanation: Versatility means ability to be used in many different situations.

questions.question: What can be inferred about why SMAW is preferred for outdoor work?
choices: It can handle windy conditions better than other processes
It does not require filler material
questions.explanation: The passage states SMAW can be used outdoors in windy conditions, implying it handles wind better.

questions.question: Which statement best describes the author’s approach to welding hazards?
choices: Balanced — acknowledges hazards and specifies protections
Alarmist — suggests welding should be avoided
Vague — does not specify any protections
questions.explanation: The author presents hazards and specific protections — a balanced approach.

questions.question: What is the 4-to-1 rule for extension ladders?
choices: Use a 4-foot ladder for every 1-foot wall
The base should be 1 foot from the wall for every 4 feet of height
Four workers should hold the ladder for every 1 on it
questions.explanation: 4-to-1 rule: base 1 foot from wall for every 4 feet of height.

questions.question: How far must an extension ladder extend above a roof landing?
questions.explanation: The ladder must extend at least 3 feet above the landing.

questions.question: What does three-point contact mean?
choices: Contact with three people holding the ladder
Two hands and one foot, or two feet and one hand on the ladder
Touching the wall at three points
questions.explanation: Two hands and one foot, or two feet and one hand on the ladder.

questions.question: Which of the following is a common cause of ladder accidents mentioned in the passage?
questions.explanation: Overreaching while on the ladder is listed as a common cause.

questions.question: A step ladder should NEVER be:
choices: Leaned against a wall like an extension ladder
questions.explanation: Step ladders should never be leaned against a wall like an extension ladder.

questions.question: What should be done with a damaged ladder?
choices: Use it for light tasks only
Take it out of service and tag it as defective
Have a supervisor approve its continued use
questions.explanation: Damaged ladders must be taken out of service and tagged as defective.

questions.question: Based on the 4-to-1 rule, if a ladder reaches 20 feet, how far from the wall should the base be?
The passage suggests that ladder safety is “fundamental yet frequently overlooked.” What can you infer?
choices: Ladder safety is well enforced everywhere
Many workers know the rules but do not always follow them
Ladders are no longer used in construction
questions.explanation: Many workers know ladder safety rules but frequently do not follow them.

questions.question: Why was asbestos widely used in building materials?
choices: It had heat resistance, strength, and insulating properties
It was available in many colors
questions.explanation: Asbestos had heat resistance, strength, and insulating properties.

questions.question: When does asbestos become dangerous according to the passage?
choices: When it is in floor tiles
When it is in good condition
When it is disturbed (cut, drilled, sanded, or demolished)
When it is in buildings older than 50 years
questions.explanation: Asbestos becomes dangerous when disturbed (cut, drilled, sanded, demolished).

questions.question: How long after exposure can asbestos-related diseases appear?
questions.explanation: Diseases may not appear until 20 to 50 years after exposure.

questions.question: What must happen before renovation of buildings constructed before 1981?
choices: An asbestos inspection must be performed
All workers must be tested for lung disease
The building must be sealed for 30 days
questions.explanation: Buildings before 1981 must have an asbestos inspection before renovation.

questions.question: Why is negative air pressure maintained during asbestos abatement?
choices: To prevent asbestos fibers from escaping the work area
To speed up the removal process
To dry the materials before removal
questions.explanation: Negative pressure prevents fibers from escaping the sealed work area.

questions.question: The word “abatement” as used in the passage most likely means:
questions.explanation: Abatement means professional removal or containment.

questions.question: What should a worker do if they suspect they have found asbestos?
choices: Continue working and report it at the end of the day
Stop work immediately and report to their supervisor
Wet it down and cover it with plastic
questions.explanation: Workers must stop work immediately and report to their supervisor.

questions.question: Which statement from the passage is an opinion?
choices: Asbestos was used from the 1920s through the 1980s
Mesothelioma is a cancer of the lung lining
None — all statements in the passage are facts
questions.explanation: All statements in the passage are factual and verifiable.

questions.question: What is 7/8 of 96?
A pipe is 18 feet 9 inches long. In inches, that is:
A crew of 4 can dig a trench in 6 hours. How long for 8 workers?
What is √196?
¾ ÷ ½ = ?
A room is 11 ft × 14 ft. How many square yards of carpet?
What is 200% of 45?
A 16-foot board is cut into pieces 2 feet 8 inches long. How many pieces?
questions.explanation: 2 ft 8 in = 32 in. 16 ft = 192 in. 192/32 = 6 pieces.

questions.question: What is the area of a triangle with base 18 ft and height 10 ft?
A tank holds 400 gallons. It is 3/4 full. How many gallons are in it?
Convert 2.75 feet to inches.
What is the volume of a cylinder with radius 2 ft and height 5 ft? (π = 3.14)
A worker makes $26/hr. Overtime is 1.5×. What is 4 hours of overtime pay?
The ratio of sand to cement is 4:1. For 50 bags total, how many bags of sand?
A floor plan shows a rectangular garage: 24 ft × 22 ft. What is the area?
A blueprint at 1/4” = 1’ shows a wall 4.5 inches long. Actual length?
A bar chart shows crew output: Mon 45, Tue 52, Wed 38, Thu 55, Fri 50. Average?
A diagram shows a concrete sidewalk: 60 ft × 4 ft × 4 in deep. Volume in cubic yards?
A materials table shows tile at $4.25/sq ft. A room on the plan is 10 ft × 12 ft. Cost?
A site plan at 1” = 50 ft shows two points 2.8 inches apart. Actual distance?
A pie chart shows a $200,000 budget: Materials 50%, Labor 30%, Equipment 20%. Labor cost?
A floor plan shows an L-shaped room: 20×15 and 10×8. Total area?
A diagram shows a circular manhole cover with a diameter of 30 inches. Area? (π=3.14)
A table shows 3 paint types: A covers 300 sq ft/gal, B covers 350 sq ft/gal, C covers 400 sq ft/gal. For 1,200 sq ft, how many gallons of B?
questions.explanation: 1200/350 = 3.43 gallons. Must round up to 4 gallons.

questions.question: A drawing shows a trench 100 ft long, 3 ft wide, 5 ft deep. Volume in cu yards?
A graph shows temperatures: 6AM=45°F, 9AM=58°F, Noon=72°F, 3PM=78°F. Rise from 6AM to 3PM?
A floor plan shows a room 16×18 with a 3×4 closet. Area excluding closet?
A scale drawing at 1” = 8 ft shows a lot 6.5” × 4”. Actual area?
A table: Lumber $4.50/ft, need 120 ft. Fasteners $32/box, need 5 boxes. Total?
A diagram shows a roof triangle: base 32 ft, height 12 ft. Area?
A 500-lb crate sits on a floor (μ = 0.5). What force starts it moving?
Two gears mesh: driver has 12 teeth, driven has 48. Gear ratio?
A fixed pulley provides a mechanical advantage of:
questions.explanation: A fixed pulley changes direction only. MA = 1.

questions.question: Ohm’s Law: V=240, I=8A. Resistance?
A 15-ft ramp rises 3 ft. MA?
Which stores elastic potential energy?
questions.explanation: A compressed spring stores elastic PE.

questions.question: Three 12Ω resistors in parallel. Total resistance?
Newton’s Second Law: 50-lb force on 10-slug mass. Acceleration?
A belt on two pulleys of equal diameter. Driven pulley speed?
Pressure = 200 psi, Area = 8 in². Force?
Object dropped from 64 ft. Using gravity = 32 ft/s², time to fall?
A wrench handle is extended with a pipe. This increases:
In a series circuit, current is:
questions.explanation: In series, current is the same through all components.

questions.question: Which is a third-class lever?
questions.explanation: Fishing rod: effort between fulcrum and load = 3rd class.

questions.question: A 10-inch driver pulley connects by belt to a 5-inch driven. Driver at 300 RPM. Driven speed?
Fluid flows from a wide pipe to a narrow pipe. Velocity in the narrow section:
questions.explanation: Continuity: fluid speeds up in narrow pipe.

questions.question: An object’s inertia depends on its:
A 100-W bulb at 120V draws how many amps?
Impulse equals:
A cross-shaped belt causes pulleys to rotate:
Work = 1,200 ft·lb, Time = 4s. Power?
An inclined plane reduces the _____ needed while increasing the _____.
questions.explanation: Inclined planes reduce force but increase distance.

questions.question: Archimedes’ principle states buoyant force equals:
questions.explanation: Buoyant force = weight of displaced fluid.

questions.question: A gear train: 10T drives 30T, which shares a shaft with 15T that drives 45T. Overall ratio?
questions.explanation: First pair: 30/10=3. Second pair: 45/15=3. Overall: 3×3 = 9:1 reduction, so 1:9.

questions.question: Kinetic friction is typically _____ static friction.
P = V × I. A 240V circuit with 20A =
What happens to PE as a ball falls?
questions.explanation: As a ball falls, PE converts to KE.

questions.question: A 40-tooth driver gear meshes with 10-tooth driven. Driver at 100 RPM. Driven speed?
questions.explanation: Ratio = 40/10 = 4. Smaller gear turns 4× faster: 100×4 = 400 RPM.

questions.question: Hydraulic systems cannot:
questions.explanation: Hydraulic systems transmit pressure but cannot create energy.

questions.question: A nail penetrates wood because the force is concentrated on a:
questions.explanation: Small area = high pressure. That’s why nails penetrate.

questions.question: Conservation of momentum: Before collision = 500 lb·ft/s. After?
A lever has 12-ft effort arm and 3-ft load arm. MA?
In a parallel circuit, total resistance is always:
choices: Greater than the largest individual resistance
Equal to the sum of all resistances
Less than the smallest individual resistance
questions.explanation: In parallel, total R is always less than the smallest individual R.

questions.question: Which material is the worst conductor?
questions.explanation: Glass is an insulator; the others are conductors.

questions.question: A screw with closer threads has _____ MA but requires _____ turns.
questions.explanation: Closer threads = more MA but more turns required.

questions.question: Two objects: A (5 kg at 10 m/s) and B (10 kg at 5 m/s). Which has more momentum?
Meshed gears rotate in _____ directions.
questions.explanation: Meshed gears rotate in opposite directions.

questions.question: A pulley system has 4 supporting ropes. To lift 800 lb:
V = IR. R = 15Ω, I = 3A. V =
Work is measured in:
questions.explanation: Work is measured in foot-pounds or joules.

questions.question: Gravity provides a constant acceleration of about:
A second-class lever has the load:
questions.explanation: Second-class: load between fulcrum and effort.

questions.question: What does a lubricant NOT do?
questions.explanation: Lubricants reduce friction, not increase it.

questions.question: An escalator moves a 150-lb person up 20 ft in 10 s. Power?
At what depth does OSHA require protective systems?
How much does one cubic yard of soil weigh approximately?
What is the difference between shoring and shielding?
choices: Shoring supports walls; shielding protects workers inside a box
Shoring is for deep trenches only
Shielding prevents collapse; shoring does not
questions.explanation: Shoring supports walls to prevent collapse; shielding protects workers inside a box.

questions.question: How far from the edge must spoil piles be kept?
When must a competent person inspect an excavation?
Why does water accumulation increase cave-in risk?
choices: Water makes soil heavier and less stable
questions.explanation: Water makes soil heavier and less stable.

questions.question: The word “spoil” as used in the passage means:
What can you infer about stable rock excavations?
choices: They are the most common type
They do not require protective systems under the 5-ft rule
questions.explanation: Stable rock excavations are exempt from the 5-ft protective system requirement.

questions.question: What is the primary function of a transformer?
choices: To change voltage levels of AC electricity
questions.explanation: Transformers change voltage levels of AC electricity.

questions.question: If the primary has 500 turns and secondary has 100 turns, the transformer:
Why is electricity transmitted at high voltages?
choices: It minimizes energy losses over long distances
High voltage is cheaper to produce
questions.explanation: High voltage minimizes energy losses over long distances.

questions.question: What is the purpose of mineral oil in transformers?
questions.explanation: Mineral oil serves as coolant and insulator.

questions.question: What are PCBs?
choices: A toxic substance in some older transformers
questions.explanation: PCBs are a toxic substance found in some older transformers.

questions.question: Standard residential voltage in the US is:
The word “induces” as used in the passage means:
What can you infer about transformers made after 1979?
choices: They generally do not contain PCBs
questions.explanation: After 1979, transformers generally do not contain PCBs.

questions.question: What is the main idea of this passage?
choices: Power tools are more dangerous than hand tools
Proper tool use and maintenance prevents workplace injuries
Workers should avoid using power tools
questions.explanation: Proper tool use and maintenance prevents injuries.

questions.question: Why should tool guards never be removed?
choices: They protect workers from contact with moving parts
They are required for warranty coverage
questions.explanation: Guards protect workers from moving parts.

questions.question: What hazard do pneumatic tools present according to the passage?
questions.explanation: Disconnected air hoses can whip dangerously.

questions.question: Using a wrench as a hammer can cause:
choices: The wrench to shatter and send metal fragments
questions.explanation: A wrench used as hammer can shatter.

questions.question: What does “double-insulated” most likely mean?
choices: The tool has two power cords
The tool has extra protection against electrical shock without a ground wire
The tool requires twice as much electricity
questions.explanation: Double-insulated = extra shock protection without ground wire.

questions.question: What should be done with damaged tools?
choices: Only use them for light work
Store them until they are needed

questions.question: GFCIs are required on construction sites because:
The author’s tone is best described as:
GFCIs primarily protect against:
How does a GFCI detect a problem?
choices: By monitoring current difference between hot and neutral wires
questions.explanation: Monitors current difference between hot and neutral.

questions.question: How quickly does a GFCI trip?
AFCIs primarily protect against:
Where are GFCIs required?
choices: In wet locations such as bathrooms, kitchens, and outdoor outlets
questions.explanation: Wet locations: bathrooms, kitchens, outdoor outlets.

questions.question: What is an arcing fault?
choices: Sparks from electricity jumping across a gap in damaged wiring
questions.explanation: Sparks from electricity jumping across a gap.

questions.question: Combination AFCI/GFCI devices provide:
choices: Both shock protection and fire protection

questions.question: What can you infer about homes built before AFCIs were required?
choices: They are unsafe to live in
They may not have arc fault protection in living areas
questions.explanation: Older homes may lack arc fault protection in living areas.

A 15-tooth gear drives a 45-tooth gear at 900 RPM. Driven speed?
Which force keeps a book resting on a table?
questions.explanation: The normal force from the table supports the book against gravity.

questions.question: A hydraulic piston: input 3 in² with 60 lb, output 30 in². Output force?

questions.explanation: Adding parallel branches decreases total R.

questions.question: An object at rest has zero:

Voltage = 60V, Resistance = 12Ω. Current?

questions.explanation: Idler reverses direction, so driver and driven turn same way.

questions.explanation: Near-frictionless surface: continues at constant velocity (1st Law).

questions.question: Contact area does NOT significantly affect:
questions.explanation: Contact area does not significantly affect friction between solid surfaces.

questions.question: P = 1,500 W, V = 120V. Current?
A compound gear train multiplies _____ while reducing _____.
questions.explanation: Compound gear trains multiply torque while reducing speed.

questions.question: Potential energy at top of a 25-ft building for a 40-lb object?
An object floats when buoyant force is:
A driver gear: 20T at 400 RPM. Driven gear: 80T. Driven speed?
Heat from friction is an example of energy:
questions.explanation: Heat from friction is energy conversion (mechanical to thermal).

questions.question: Series circuit: one bulb burns out. Others?
questions.explanation: Series: one failure breaks the whole circuit.

questions.question: An object’s velocity changes from 20 ft/s to 50 ft/s in 6 s. Acceleration?
A fixed pulley’s main advantage is:
questions.explanation: Fixed pulley changes direction of force.

questions.question: Coefficient of friction: 0.6. Normal force: 400 lb. Friction force?
Power = Work / Time. Same work, half the time means:
questions.explanation: Same work in half time = double the power.

questions.question: Electrical conductors include all EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Dry wood is an insulator; the others conduct.

questions.question: A screw with fine threads vs coarse threads: fine threads have:
questions.explanation: Fine threads have more MA but require more turns.

questions.question: A 50-lb force + 30-lb force in the same direction. Net force?
Hydraulic fluid is essentially:
A seesaw is what class lever?
Ohm’s Law: doubling resistance while halving voltage. Current?
questions.explanation: Original I=V/R. New I=(V/2)/(2R)=V/4R=original/4. Current is quartered.

questions.question: An 800-lb object moving at 5 ft/s hits a wall over 0.5s. Impact force?
Rolling friction is less than sliding friction because:
choices: The contact area is constantly changing
Friction only acts on flat surfaces
questions.explanation: Rolling: contact area constantly changes, reducing deformation resistance.

questions.question: Parallel circuit: each branch has _____ voltage.
questions.explanation: Parallel: same voltage across all branches.

questions.question: A wheel and axle: larger wheel provides _____ at the axle.
questions.explanation: Larger wheel produces more force at the axle.

questions.question: Energy cannot be _____ or _____.
questions.explanation: Energy cannot be created or destroyed.

questions.question: A car brakes to a stop. KE converts mostly to:
questions.explanation: Braking converts KE to heat (thermal energy).

questions.question: At what height does OSHA require fall protection on construction sites?
Why are guardrails the preferred fall protection method?
choices: They prevent falls without requiring worker action
questions.explanation: Guardrails prevent falls without requiring worker action.

questions.question: What is the required height of a standard top rail?
What must happen to fall arrest equipment after it is used in a fall?
choices: It can continue to be used
It must be inspected and reused
It must be removed from service
It must be cleaned and stored

questions.question: An anchor point must support at least:

To prevent tools and materials from falling off the edge
To mark the edge of the work surface

questions.question: A fall protection plan must be:
What is rigging?
choices: The process of safely moving heavy loads with lifting equipment
questions.explanation: Rigging is the process of safely moving heavy loads.

questions.question: Why is sling angle important?
choices: Lower angles increase sling tension dramatically
Higher angles increase the load weight

questions.question: At a 30-degree sling angle, tension is _____ the vertical load.

The number of workers on site

questions.question: A 10,000-lb rated sling at 30 degrees can safely hold:
Defective rigging should be:

choices: To prevent someone from accidentally using unsafe equipment
It is too expensive to repair
questions.explanation: To prevent accidental reuse of unsafe equipment.

choices: When adopted by a state or local jurisdiction

The NEC is only available online
Training materials are required by OSHA

questions.question: The passage describes the NEC as a “model code.” This means:
choices: It is the best code ever written
It serves as a template that jurisdictions can adopt
It applies only to model homes

questions.question: What can you infer about states that modify NEC provisions?

They may face unique local conditions that require different standards
They do not follow any electrical code
